metrics.narrative: The RFP briefing on 12 Jan 2026 quantified scope (Duncan Tennyson: ANZ indirect spend of roughly $110M via POs and $130M non-PO annually, 10 power users and ~200 business users), and the 2 Jul 2025 demo surfaced a concrete pain point (a buyer speaker: 'there is a need to manage one budget across multiple vendors within the requisition and purchase order system,' with a stated risk of budget over-utilization going undetected). No Zycus-specific ROI or savings figure has been quantified back to TTI yet.

champion.narrative: Tej Ram is the most consistently engaged contact -- coordinating references (Nissan, TataPlay, completed by 30 Jun 2026), responding directly to a Zycus deal-team member about process status, and relaying the CFO's scope timeline. Duncan Tennyson runs the RFP process day-to-day and is described internally as 'more technical evaluator' (6 Jun 2026 note). Neither has documented access to, or advocacy with, the CFO -- the deal has no confirmed champion at decision-maker level, only strong technical/procurement-level engagement.

competition.narrative: Coupa, GEP, iValua and SAP Ariba were all named as competitors during the RFP (13 Jan 2026: 'vs Coupa/iValua/Ariba'). TTI down-selected to a final two vendors on 11 May 2026, with GEP and iValua flagged that same day as 'strong threats.' By 1 Jul 2026, only Coupa is referenced as the live comparison point ('Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' from the S2C scope pivot), suggesting Coupa is very likely the other finalist. Zycus is currently behind on RFP deliverables: GEP, iValua and Coupa had already given TTI a RACI and phased implementation plan as of 1 Jul 2026, which Zycus had not yet matched.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated: manual, disconnected budget tracking across multiple vendors and cost centers (2 Jul 2025 demo, buyer speaker: 'managing a single budget for a project that involves multiple vendors and purchase requests presents challenges in tracking budget utilization'); manual invoice-matching pain addressed via the relevant stakeholder in the demo; and, critically, the 1 Jul 2026 note states the entire S2P-to-S2C scope pivot is 'coming from the risk they feel moving from ECC to S4Hana' -- i.e., TTI's live SAP ECC6-to-S/4HANA migration (design starting Q3 2026 per the 12 Jan 2026 briefing) is now the dominant driver of their buying behavior.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or Order Form discussion is on record. The deal is still pre-selection (Shortlisted, awaiting the CFO's scope/vendor call the week of 13 Jul 2026); contracting mechanics have not yet been raised in any Next Step entry or Avoma touchpoint.

economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO (Melbourne-based) is named as the deal's economic buyer: the 12 Dec 2025 call recorded 'CFO wants tender issued before year-end' and the 1 Jul 2026 Next Step notes 'They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decided on scope.' However, the CFO has never had direct or confirmed indirect engagement with Zycus; an attempt to meet the CPO in Melbourne on 3 Feb 2026 was blocked due to a conflict-of-interest rule during the RFP process (per the 22 Jan / 10 Feb 2026 notes), and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is recorded as false. A hand-typed field (X10a_Sponsor__c) claims 'we have CPO and majority of procurement on our side,' but this is rep-reported, not buyer-confirmed, and does not name a CFO relationship.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well documented across a year of Next Step entries: RFP release (Jan 2026) -> written proposal (submitted 12 Feb 2026) -> onsite demo (10 Mar 2026) -> clarification round (18-23 Mar 2026) -> BAFO/decision dates repeatedly moved (8 May -> 29 May -> ongoing) -> down-select to two vendors (11 May 2026) -> reference checks (completed 30 Jun 2026) -> now a CFO scope-and-vendor decision the week of 13 Jul 2026. The timeline has slipped at almost every stage.

decision_criteria.narrative: The 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing call laid out explicit evaluation criteria in detail: Duncan Tennyson stated TTI would assess 'completeness of the submission,' get SMEs to review technical/functional fit, check references, and weigh the implementation program ('how would you go about implementing the solution... what does a successful implementation... phase, sequence'), resourcing needs, and full commercial terms (license, implementation, ongoing support/SLAs).

Luke's proactively offering to walk Tej and Duncan through it in person while he's in Melbourne this week — the ball is now in TTI's court to review and decide whether an in-person or phone follow-up is warranted.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-01-20 (~17.5 months in pipeline). In Shortlisted stage since the 11 May 2026 down-select to 2 (~57 days in stage as of 2026-07-07). Close date 2026-07-30 is 23 days out but sits only ~17 days after the CFO's scope decision (week of 13 Jul), with zero contracting/paperwork activity logged, making the current close date implausible even in a win scenario.

Next_Step__c (2026-06-30): Tej Ram confirmed references complete, process in final stages
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is one of TTI's final two vendors and has cleared reference checks, but a stalled deliverable (revised S2C pricing + RACI/project plan) risks leaving Zycus under-prepared for the CFO's scope/vendor decision the week of 13 Jul 2026.

flags: Close the RACI/project-plan gap before the CFO's scope call: GEP, iValua and Coupa already gave TTI a phased implementation RACI (per 1 Jul 2026); delivering Zycus's own before the week of 13 Jul 2026 is the single highest-leverage move left.

Champion advocacy unproven: Tej Ram is responsive and provides RFP access, but no call evidence shows him advocating for Zycus vs competitors or connecting us to the CFO — confirm Tej's internal position and develop a second champion (Duncan or Sonia) with coaching on how to sell Merlin AI to the CFO
No quantified business case or ROI (savings, cycle time, headcount) captured; CFO decision will require financial justification and Zycus has not built one.

Forecast indefensible: Best Case with CFO unmapped, no contract/signature activity, close date 30 Jul precedes realistic contracting timeline post-13-Jul award; will slip to Aug-Sep 2026 (Next Step 15 Jun, 1 Jul 2026)
Scope pivot to S2C-only (50% likelihood) will halve deal size from $220k to ~$110-130k and defer P2P/Merlin Intake revenue to 2027.
Forecast is Best Case but deal lacks Economic Buyer engagement, champion, and submission readiness; should be Pipeline
Lean into the S4HANA migration angle: position Zycus's phased S2C-first roadmap as the safer path through TTI's SAP ECC6-to-S4HANA transition, directly countering the risk driving the scope cut.
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage gap is the missing RACI/implementation plan that three named rivals already delivered, followed closely by the total absence of CFO engagement ahead of the imminent scope decision.

customer_preference.evidence: Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram told Zycus on 1 Jul 2026 that shifting the scope to Source-to-Contract only puts Zycus in a stronger position versus Coupa -- a buyer-voiced lean, but conditional on revised pricing and the CFO's decision the week of 13 Jul 2026, not a confirmed selection.

competitive_position.summary: TTI ran a formal RFP naming Coupa, GEP, iValua and SAP Ariba as alternatives. GEP and iValua were flagged as strong threats at the 11 May 2026 down-select to two finalists, but by 1 Jul 2026 only Coupa remains the explicit comparison point, and TTI's likely scope pivot to S2C-only was described by the buyer as strengthening Zycus's position specifically against Coupa (inference: Coupa is very likely the other finalist). Zycus is currently behind on a documented deliverable (RACI/phased implementation plan) that all three named rivals already supplied.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Shortlisted, down-selected to a final two as of 11 May 2026, and currently live: a genuine two-way buyer touch 8 days ago (1 Jul 2026) and a rep response 1 day ago (8 Jul 2026). But the CFO who owns the imminent scope-and-vendor call (week of 13 Jul 2026) has never been engaged, and Zycus is behind the other finalist on a documented RFP deliverable (RACI/project plan), with 21 days left to the 30 Jul 2026 close date and no contracting activity yet.

deal_risk.text: Unmapped, unengaged economic buyer deciding imminently -- the CFO decides scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026 (about 4 days from today) with zero recorded Zycus engagement, direct or indirect.
TTI's SAP ECC6-to-S/4HANA migration (design starting Q3 2026, go-live 2027+, per the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing) is the stated driver of TTI's risk-aversion and scope contraction toward S2C-only.
Integration gatekeeper Brett Campbell (EAI Manager) has no recorded direct engagement with Zycus despite being a named contact role on the deal.
Deal timeline has slipped repeatedly since March 2026 while the Salesforce close date remains fixed at 30 Jul 2026, well before the CFO's own decision date.

win_position.text: Down-selected to the final two vendors -- TTI confirmed by email on 11 May 2026 that Zycus was shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands-on sandbox testing.
Buyer-voiced competitive tailwind -- Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram told Zycus on 1 Jul 2026 that narrowing the deal to Source-to-Contract only puts Zycus in a stronger position versus Coupa.

Economic buyer never engaged -- the Melbourne-based CFO, who requested the original RFP (12 Dec 2025 note) and will decide scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026, has had no confirmed direct or indirect Zycus contact; an attempt to meet the CPO in Melbourne on 3 Feb 2026 was blocked by a conflict-of-interest rule during the RFP process.

Behind on RFP deliverables the CFO will weigh -- GEP, iValua and Coupa had already supplied TTI a RACI and phased implementation plan as of the 1 Jul 2026 Next Step note, just ahead of the CFO's decision.

Scope-contraction risk -- the deal may shrink from the full $220k, 8-product Source-to-Pay scope to Source-to-Contract only (roughly half the value), with P2P and Merlin Intake deferred to next year, per the 1 Jul 2026 note.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer engagement 8 days ago -- Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram proactively told Zycus on 1 Jul 2026 that TTI is leaning to S2C-only and asked for revised pricing and implementation costing.
Rep sent revised S2C pricing to Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram on 8 Jul 2026 (1 day ago) and targeted a same-day feedback call with Tej Ram; a confirmed reply is still awaited as of this sweep.
Close date (30 Jul 2026, 21 days out) sits after the CFO's scope/vendor decision (week of 13 Jul 2026) with no contracting activity logged; the date is likely to slip into Aug-Sep 2026.
Recurring slippage pattern -- vendor-decision dates have moved from 8 May 2026 to 29 May 2026 and are now pending a CFO call, while Shortlisted stage entry was 3 Mar 2026, roughly 128 days ago.

customer_commitment.text: TTI provided two references (Nissan and TataPlay) by 30 Jun 2026 and confirmed reference checks are complete, moving the deal into final-stage evaluation.
No sandbox testing or detailed integration deep-dive session has been confirmed yet, despite being flagged as the next step since 25 May 2026.
